motivation: The user needed to understand what the project actually is and how it’s structured/bootstrapped, rather than treating it as a simple Anthropic-API CLI, to guide further reading and potential modifications.
approach: Performed a top-down architecture walkthrough (build/entrypoint → main orchestrator → query engine/tool loop) and mapped core subsystems (context, tools/commands/skills, multi-provider API layer, MCP/plugins, memory, native extensions), plus a quick executable/version smoke check on the built CLI.
challenges: The repo is large and monolithic with complex feature/environment matrices and some duplicate “mirror” source trees that add reading noise and can mislead navigation.
tradeoffs: Validation was limited to build/output executability (e.g., `--version`) rather than running full tests or exercising all modes, favoring fast orientation over comprehensive verification.
outcome: Produced a concrete mental model of the system as a full Claude Code-like product (CLI/TUI + agent loop + tools + plugins/MCP + memory + multi-backend API) with identified main execution paths and recommended reading order, and confirmed the dist CLI runs under both Bun and Node.
